---
title: "SCANDARE-ORL : CNV QC"
author: "Abderaouf Hamza"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    theme: united
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

<!--
================================================================================
 Script  : 00_CNV_QC.Rmd
 Project : SCANDARE-ORL (HNSCC intra-tumour heterogeneity study)
 Purpose : Quality control of FACETS copy-number calls produced by the DRAGON
           pipeline, and computation of NRPCC (number of reads per chromosome
           copy) as defined in Dentro et al.
 Inputs  : - Sample list (TSV) with one row per region: ID_SCANDARE,
             Barcode_DRAGON, ...
           - Per-run DRAGON output directories containing FACETS
             `cnv_report.table2.tsv` and `mapping.stats.xlsx` files.
 Outputs : - TSV table with one row per region containing:
             homdel_frac, n_seg, purity, ploidy, mean_cov_x, nrpcc, and
             pass/fail QC flags.
 Method  : Adapted from the facets-preview / facets-qc QC criteria
           (https://github.com/taylor-lab/facets-preview). Several QC checks
           had to be omitted because the DRAGON FACETS output does not
           expose `mafR` (minor-allele log-odds ratio) nor `cf.em`
           (EM cell fraction).
 Usage   : Knit interactively from RStudio after editing the CONFIG block.
================================================================================
-->

```{r setup}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  cache   = FALSE
)

library(DT)
library(janitor)
library(kableExtra)
library(vroom)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
```

# Configuration

Edit these paths before running.

```{r config}
# --- CONFIG -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sample list (TSV) with one row per sequenced region.
sample_list_path <- "<path/to/SCANDARE_ORL_sample_list.tsv>"

# Root directory containing per-run DRAGON output folders.
dragon_data_dir  <- "<path/to/dragon_runs>"

# Output TSV (QC table with one row per region).
output_qc_path   <- "<path/to/results/seq_cnv_qc_SCANDARE.tsv>"
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

# Objectives

QC of FACETS CNV calls. QC criteria are inspired from the
[facets-preview](https://bandla-chai.gitbook.io/facets-preview/reviewing-fits)
module
[facets-qc](https://github.com/taylor-lab/facets-preview/blob/master/facets_qc/v1.0/facets_fit_qc.R).
NRPCC is computed as in Dentro et al.

# Method

```{r function}
# generate_facet_qc() ----------------------------------------------------------
# Compute per-sample QC metrics from a FACETS segmentation table.
# A simplified version of facets-qc: the DRAGON FACETS output lacks
# `mafR` and `cf.em` so the corresponding checks are omitted.
generate_facet_qc <- function(cnv_table) {

  total_genome_size <- sum(cnv_table$Stop - cnv_table$Start)
  ploidy <- unique(cnv_table$Ploidy)
  purity <- unique(cnv_table$Cellularity)[1]
  n_seg  <- nrow(cnv_table)

  homdel_table <- cnv_table[cnv_table$Copy == 0, ]
  homdel_frac  <- sum(homdel_table$Stop - homdel_table$Start, na.rm = TRUE) /
                  total_genome_size

  tibble(
    homdel_frac = homdel_frac,
    n_seg       = n_seg,
    purity      = purity,
    ploidy      = ploidy
  )
}
```

# Data

Segmentation files from FACETS (DRAGON pipeline output).

```{r data-import}
SCANDARE_sample_list.tsv <-
  read_tsv(sample_list_path)

SCANDARE_sample_list <-
  SCANDARE_sample_list.tsv %>%
  mutate(
    # Some samples have two DRAGON barcodes corresponding to two runs (re-run
    # after a first failure). The most recent is the second one and is kept.
    Barcode_DRAGON = map(Barcode_DRAGON, ~ str_split(.x, "/")[[1]][1]),
    Run_Barcode    = str_extract(Barcode_DRAGON, "D[:digit:]{3}")
  )

concat_cnv_table <-
  tibble(
    cnv_reports_paths = list.files(
      dragon_data_dir,
      pattern    = "cnv_report.table2.tsv",
      recursive  = TRUE,
      full.names = TRUE
    ),
    Run_Barcode = str_extract(cnv_reports_paths, "D[:digit:]{3}")
  ) %>%
  right_join(SCANDARE_sample_list) %>%
  mutate(cnv_report = map2(cnv_reports_paths, Barcode_DRAGON, ~ {
    read_tsv(.x) %>%
      filter(Barcode == .y)
  })) %>%
  unnest(cnv_report)

concat_qc_table <-
  tibble(
    qc_mapping_paths = list.files(
      dragon_data_dir,
      pattern    = "mapping.stats.xlsx",
      recursive  = TRUE,
      full.names = TRUE
    ),
    qc_mapping_file = map(qc_mapping_paths,
                          ~ clean_names(read_xlsx(.x, col_types = "text")))
  ) %>%
  unnest(qc_mapping_file) %>%
  type_convert()
```

# Generate per-sample QC

```{r generate-qc}
facet_qcs_SCANDARE <-
  concat_cnv_table %>%
  group_by(Barcode) %>%
  nest() %>%
  transmute(facet_qcs = map(data, ~ generate_facet_qc(.x))) %>%
  unnest(facet_qcs) %>%
  left_join(select(concat_qc_table, "Barcode" = "barcode", mean_cov_x)) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # NRPCC: number of reads per chromosome copy (Dentro et al., 2021).
    nrpcc              = mean_cov_x * purity / (purity * ploidy + (1 - purity) * 2),
    # purity == 0.3 is FACETS's default fallback when the fit fails.
    filter_purity      = !is.na(purity) & purity != 0.3,
    filter_homdel_frac = homdel_frac <= 0.05
  )
```

# Write output

```{r write-out}
write_tsv(facet_qcs_SCANDARE, output_qc_path)
```
